IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: LINC HOUSING CORPORATION IS A NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION THAT BUILDS, OWNS AND OPERATES AFFORDABLE HOUSING FOR SENIORS AND FAMILIES THROUGHOUT CALIFORNIA. LINC ALSO OFFERS ITS RESIDENTS LIFE-ENHANCING SERVICES THROUGH THE LINC CARES PROGRAM.
IRS990.MissionDesc: LINCs purpose, as specified in its bylaws is, directly or indirectly, to create, own, operate, manage, provide assistance to or procure low and moderate cost housing with or without supportive social services or assistance with the tasks of daily living, to lessen the burdens of government, and to combat community deterioration.
IRS990.Desc: LINC Housing Corporation is a nonprofit organization that builds, owns and operates affordable housing for seniors and families throughout California. LINC is committed to creating and preserving housing that is affordable, environmentally sustainable, and a catalyst for community improvement.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: LINC HOUSING CORPORATION offers its residents life-enhancing services through the LINC Cares program.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part II-B, Line 1i - Other Activities Description
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: part II-B, LINE 1AHeld one phone bank with 15 volunteers. part II-B, LINE 1DDigital support letters to legislators: Sign-On: PBV Cap Flexibility Sign-On: HCD Service Cap Letter Sign-On: Budget letter to CA Legislature advocating for funding programsSupport letters and emails to legislative staff for California AB 1657, AB 1932, and SB 393Support letter to LA County Board of Supervisors for creation of new LA County Homelessness Deptpart II-B, LINE 1FMonetary contributions of $25,000 to LA County Measure A Campaign and $3,000 to State Prop 5 Campaign.PART II-B, LINE 1GMeeting with staff from Office of CA Asm Lowenthal about development in the district in May 2024Roundtable with CA Asm Wicks on topic of housing in Sept 2024Property tour with US Representative Linda Sanchez in Oct 2024

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part V, Line 4: Intended uses of the endowment fund.
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The fund is managed by Long Beach Community FOundation (LBCF) and California Community Foundation (CCF). The funds shall be operarated as an endowment fund subjected to the spending policy of LBCF and CCF. The objectives of the Fund are to create growth while enabling an annual draw down. LBCF's mission is to initiate positive change for Long Beach through charitiable giving, stewardship, and strategic grantmaking. As a key player in the CIty of Long Beach where LINC's offices have been located for decades, there are many benefits of partnership with LBCF in general and becoming a fund holder specifically.LBCF is well connected with local leaders, philanthropic intiatives, and weathy donors in Long Beach area. Having an account gives LINC visibility with these connections. WHen LINC establishes an LBCF account, the foundation will introduce LINC to their donors at new fund holder events. LBCF will also share LINC's events, news, fundraisers, volunteer opportunities, etc. with their network so social media and beyond and direct donors to LINC as a giving option for the donor advised funds. LBCF initiatives provide visibility to the wider Long Beach community for nonprofits. A relationship wioth LBCF also provides LINC with personal introductions to important players in Long Beach for local business opportunities for housing and community development as LINC works to "go deep" in this communities.CCF mission is to invest in, partner in, and amplify the power of community toward a good life. CCF enables partnerships, investments and champion advocacy for reforms that multiply impact for communities. Recognizing that community members are their own best advocates, they connect those who need support with those committed to driving progress.

PART X,LINE 2: LINC HOUSING CORPORATION IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE (THE "CODE"), AND IS EXEMPT FROM STATE INCOME TAXES UNDER THE COMPARABLE STATE TAX CODE, AS CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS WHEREBY ONLY UNRELATED INCOME, AS DEFINED BY SECTION 512(A)(1) OF THE CODE, IS SUBJECT TO FEDERAL INCOME TAX. BOTH ENTITIES FILE AN EXEMPT ORGANIZATION RETURN IN THE U.S. FEDERAL JURISDICTION AND THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA. THE ORGANIZATION FOLLOWS THE ACCOUNTING STANDARD FOR CONTINGENCIES IN EVALUATING UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS. THE GUIDANCE PRESCRIBES RECOGNITION THRESHOLD PRINCIPLES FOR FINANICAL STATEMENT RECOGNITION OF TAX POSITIONS TAKEN OR EXPECTED TO BE TAKEN ON A TAX RETURN THAT ARE NOT CERTAIN TO BE REALIZED. NO LIABILITY HAS BEEN RECOGNIZED BY THE ORGANIZATION FOR UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2024 AND 2023.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Neighbors for Aff Hsg and hom

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Grantmaker's Description of How Grants are Used
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE GRANTS WERE MADE TO OTHER 501 (C)(3) ORGANIZATIONS WHICH EXEMPT PURPOSE IS TO FURTHER THE CAUSE OF AFFORDABLE HOUSING FOR LOW-INCOME PERSONS. The organization reviews the financial statements of the recipient organization to ensure that the funds are used for their intended purpose.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The form 990 is reviewed and approved by the President & CEO prior to filing.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL Officers, DIRECTORS, COMMITTEE MEMBERS AND STAFF MUST COMPLY with the conflict of interest policy included in THE company'S employee's Handbook. IN ADDITION, the conflict of interest policy is included in the COMPANY'S "guiding principles of board service" which EACH board MEMBER is required to ADHERE TO.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Every three years a compensation salary survey is completed based on salary surveys prepared by an outside consulting firm. All proposed compensation for each position is presented first to the Operations Committee for their recommendation and then to the entire board of directors for approval.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION MAKES COPIES OF ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND PROVIDES THEM TO THE PUBLIC UPON WRITTEN REQUEST.
INCREASE DUE TO BOOKING WHOLLY OWNED PARTNERSHIPS = $1308691
INCREASE DUE TO INV IN K-1s, SEE SCH O = -$21630

THE TAX RETURN HAS BEEN PREPARED TO INCLUDE LINC HOUSING CORPORATION'S ACTIVITIES PLUS THEIR INVESTMENT IN PARTNERSHIPS PER THEIR 2024 K-1S SO THAT THE PARTNERSHIP ACTIVITY CORRECTLY REFLECTS THE ORGANIZATION'S PERCENTAGE INTEREST IN THE PARTNERSHIPS. THE SCHEDULE K-1 ITEMS OF INCOME AND EXPENSE ARE ON TAX BASIS AND THE AUDIT IS ON GAAP BASIS, THERE IS AN ADJUSTMENT TO CORRECT FUND BALANCE DUE TO TAX AND GAAP DIFFERENCES FROM THE ORGANIZATION'S PARTNERSHIP INVESTMENTS AS FOLLOWS:BEGINNING FUND BALANCE $ 101,890,741.CURRENT YEAR NET INCOME $ 33,173,362.INCREASE/(DECREASE) DUE TO GAAP/TAX DIFFERENCES FROM WHOLLY OWNED PARTNERSHIP FUND BAL $ 1,308,691. INVESTMENT IN PARTNERSHIPS PER K-1s $ -21,630. ENDING FUND BALANCE $ 136,351,164.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: LINC HOUSING - HUNTINGTON PARK APTS LLC

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HSG TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS
PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HSG TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS
